---
title: "Nick Lewis"
image: profile.jpg # Replace with your downloaded LinkedIn profile pic
about:
  template: jolla
  image-width: 15em
  image-shape: round
---

<div class="banner-container">
  <img src="banner.png" class="banner-image"> </div>

::: {.button-rectangle}
[GIS Portfolio](https://github.com/ntlewis1251) [Resume (PDF)](CV.pdf)
:::

**GIS Analyst / Geologist** Richmond, VA | ntompkinslew@gmail.com | (757) 667-8823  

---

## EDUCATION
**Bachelor of Arts and Sciences in Geology, Minor in Data Science** | William & Mary  
[cite_start]*May 2026* W&M Stofan Scholarship, William & Mary Geology and Elle Stofan [cite: 7, 8, 10]

## TECHNICAL EXPERIENCE

### Geotechnical Engineering Intern
**GET Solutions, Inc., a Terracon Company** | June - August 2024  
* [cite_start]Performed soil testing using multiple techniques, including sample collection by hand auger and from drilling operations. [cite: 16]
* [cite_start]Operational, field-level groundwater and environmental testing. [cite: 17]
* [cite_start]Selected for an intern conference in Charlotte, NC. [cite: 18]
* [cite_start]Experienced office work and computational work related to geotechnical engineering. [cite: 19]
* **References:** Dave Ferko, Project Manager; [cite_start]Greg Lewis, Project Manager. [cite: 20]

### Honors Thesis Author
**William & Mary** | "Potential causes of clustered knickpoints in the Appalachian Mountains"  
* [cite_start]Scientific writing and figure making for the Geology department. [cite: 23]
* [cite_start]Working with large (>1k items) datasets and manipulating large $(>200k~km^{2})$ rasters. [cite: 24, 25]
* [cite_start]Experience writing proposals, presenting results, and organizing large coding projects. [cite: 25, 26]

### Creator | Stream Analysis for Geoscientists
[cite_start]**PyCogs** * Educational resource for geoscientists using Python for stream analysis. [cite: 29]
* [cite_start]Experience creating Jupyter Notebooks and communicating complex information. [cite: 30]

### Panelist | Bringing Coding to the Geoscience Classroom
[cite_start]**National Association of Geoscience Teachers** * Assisted geoscience teachers in computational education. [cite: 33]

## SKILLS

| Category | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Software/Languages** | [cite_start]Python, ARCGIS, QGIS, gINT, C, Objective C, VSCode, Jupyter Notebooks [cite: 35] |
| **Physical Tools** | [cite_start]Proctor, CBR, Liquid Limit (Casagrande), Concrete compression, Soil core analysis, Well depth, Percolation, VOC testing, Petrographic microscope [cite: 35] |
| **Python Proficiencies** | [cite_start]Matplotlib, Seaborn, Pandas, GeoPandas, NumPy, Math, SciKitLearn [cite: 35] |
| **Communication** | [cite_start]Scientific writing, funding proposals, stratigraphic column/core log creation (ASTM/VDOT) [cite: 35] |

## ACTIVITIES
* [cite_start]**WCWM 90.9 FM** (2022-2026) [cite: 41]
* **G.R.A.V.E.L. [cite_start]Lab** (2024-2026) [cite: 41]
* [cite_start]**History Club** (2022-2026) [cite: 41]
* [cite_start]**SKIFFY / Tabletop Club** (2022-2026) [cite: 41]
* [cite_start]**Charles Center Research Symposium Presenter** (2026) [cite: 41]
